name: Enterprise Privacy Policy Engine (EPPE)
headline: High-traffic ASP.NET Core 8 + Angular 18 portal managing Medicaid privacy policy workflows for the federal government.
problem: EPPE needed a secure, compliant, custom workflow engine for Data Use Agreement (DUA) policies — with role-based approvals, media-dispositioning, and automated notifications — built on ASP.NET Core 8, Entity Framework Core, and an Angular 18 SPA, with zero-downtime deployments to a Kubernetes cluster.
impact: Redesigned global features — QuestPDF generation, advanced server-side search and filtering, and Hangfire-scheduled DUA-policy notifications at 15 / 30 / 45 / 60-day intervals.
Shipped role-based workflow enhancements supporting complex approval hierarchies and fine-grained policy-based authorization.
Delivered Docker + EKS deployments with GitHub Actions and Jenkins pipelines, achieving zero-downtime release cycles and tag-based releases via Helm.

name: Universal Navigation (UNAV) — NY State Portals
headline: Reusable Angular component library for standardized navigation across ~8,000 sub-applications of New York State (DOT, DMV, CSEA, and more).
problem: New York's government web ecosystem spanned thousands of sub-applications — many on aging ASP.NET Web Forms — with inconsistent navigation, branding, and accessibility. A single Angular component system, consumable from .NET back-ends and legacy Razor templates, was needed.
impact: Core contributor to an Angular-driven design system rolled out across ~8,000 sub-applications.
Built reusable Angular components served from a shared CDN and integrated into ASP.NET Core 6 Razor Pages and legacy Web Forms hosts.
Delivered .NET resource-based localization and Angular i18n + WCAG 2.1 compliance across DOT, DMV, and CSEA portals.
Mentored 12 .NET developers on ASP.NET Core, EF Core, and Angular component design.

name: CALTRANS — .NET & Angular Modernization
client: State of California Department of Transportation
headline: Full-lifecycle modernization of large-scale ASP.NET portals and Angular front-ends for the California DOT.
problem: CALTRANS was running a mix of aging ASP.NET MVC, classic ASP, and AngularJS portals that needed architectural modernization to ASP.NET Core, ADA/WCAG remediation, and a production-support capability for Sev-1 incidents.
impact: Owned full SDLC — requirements, architecture, development, NUnit + Selenium testing, deployment, and 24/7 production support.
Migrated AngularJS screens to Angular 9 components consuming an ASP.NET Core 5 Web API.
Delivered ADA/WCAG 2.0 & 2.1 remediation across .NET, Razor, and Angular assets.
Authored AS-IS / TO-BE technical docs, architecture diagrams, ERDs, and gap analyses; implemented Jenkins + Azure DevOps CI/CD across DEV, QA, UAT, and PROD.

name: ASP.NET Core Microservices + Angular Dashboards for CFO Stakeholders
headline: Containerized ASP.NET Core 5/6 microservices with RabbitMQ + Kafka messaging, served by Angular 11 SPAs and Blazor Server dashboards.
problem: Moody's needed performant, scalable financial summary dashboards backed by a fleet of .NET microservices, with CMS-driven content publishing, identity federation, and accessibility standards across a large portfolio of internal apps.
impact: Delivered Angular 11 SPAs and Blazor Server dashboards consuming ASP.NET Core gRPC + REST microservices.
Containerized .NET 5 → 6 services with Docker, orchestrated on AKS, and integrated RabbitMQ + Kafka for event-driven workflows.
Led performance work that reduced API response time ~25% via SQL partitioning, output caching, and Redis-backed query caching.
Served as Accessibility SME — achieved ADA / WCAG 2.1 compliance across web apps, generated PDFs, and Office documents.

name: NYDOCCS — Web Forms → ASP.NET Core 6 + Angular 16 Migration
client: State of New York — Department of Corrections
headline: Replatformed legacy ASP.NET Web Forms portals to ASP.NET Core 6 with Angular 16 SPAs under Octopus-gated CI/CD.
problem: NYDOCCS' web ecosystem included Web Forms portals that had drifted out of support, with limited automated testing and brittle release processes — needing a Clean-Architecture redesign on .NET Core with full Angular front-ends and modern OIDC.
impact: Directed migration of legacy Web Forms components to ASP.NET Core 6 + Angular 16 SPAs with Clean Architecture.
Stood up xUnit + Moq + Playwright test suites and integrated them into Azure DevOps pipelines.
Implemented OIDC-based authorization workflows and state/federal compliance controls.
Owned end-to-end CI/CD on Azure DevOps with Octopus Deploy promotion gates across DEV, QA, UAT, PROD.

name: San Bernardino — NGINX/PHP → ASP.NET Core on IIS Modernization
client: State of California — County of San Bernardino (ITSD)
headline: Replatformed multiple county portals (Register-to-Vote, eDocs, Worker Payroll, Affidavit, ROV Intranet) to ASP.NET Core on IIS, with Azure-hosted environments and a brand-new CI/CD foundation.
problem: San Bernardino ITSD needed to retire fragile PHP/NGINX-hosted portals and bring them onto a single, supportable ASP.NET Core stack on IIS — with Azure-managed environments, gRPC + REST microservices, and a repeatable CI/CD pipeline that the in-house team could maintain.
impact: Reverse-engineered legacy code into .NET Core 6 and upgraded to .NET 8 LTS with minimal downtime.
Modularized monoliths into standalone services and Global API-first microservices using gRPC + REST.
Set up Azure Dev / Test / UAT / Prod environments from scratch with Azure Pipelines, automated NUnit + Playwright testing, and deployment tracking.
Modernized AngularJS screens to Angular 14+ components served from the new ASP.NET Core hosts.
